machinery_genes.quote: A biochemical analysis was performed on three components: EscN, a predicted cytoplasmic ATPase; EscV, a predicted inner membrane protein; and EscC, a predicted outer membrane secretin.
machinery_genes.note: EscC = SctC-family outer-membrane secretin of the LEE T3SS. Gauthier 2003 studied the EPEC E2348/69 LEE; the LEE injectisome is conserved in C. rodentium ICC168 (Petty 2010, ROD locus ROD_29641-ROD_30191). Pallen 2005 lists EscC among the esc apparatus genes with a Yersinia (YscC) homologue.

machinery_genes.quote: Components of the secretion apparatus that had obvious homologues in the better-characterized plasmid-encoded Ysc-Yop T3SS of Yersinia sp. were given the prefix Esc and the same suffix as their Yersinia homologues
machinery_genes.note: Esc-prefix convention covers EscC, EscD, EscJ, EscN, EscR, EscS, EscT, EscU and EscV (Pallen 2005, EPEC LEE). EscD = SctD-family inner-membrane ring (YscD homologue). LEE conserved in C. rodentium ICC168 (Petty 2010).

machinery_genes.quote: Components of the secretion apparatus that had obvious homologues in the better-characterized plasmid-encoded Ysc-Yop T3SS of Yersinia sp. were given the prefix Esc and the same suffix as their Yersinia homologues
machinery_genes.note: EscJ = SctJ-family inner ring (YscJ homologue), part of the esc apparatus gene set named by Pallen 2005. LEE conserved in C. rodentium ICC168 (Petty 2010).

machinery_genes.quote: The escN gene encodes an ATPase essential for type III secretion
machinery_genes.note: Named directly in the C. rodentium type III secretome paper (Deng 2010). EscN = SctN ATPase; also studied biochemically as a cytoplasmic ATPase in EPEC by Gauthier 2003 (PMID 12761113).

machinery_genes.quote: Components of the secretion apparatus that had obvious homologues in the better-characterized plasmid-encoded Ysc-Yop T3SS of Yersinia sp. were given the prefix Esc and the same suffix as their Yersinia homologues
machinery_genes.note: EscR = SctR-family export-apparatus gate (YscR homologue), one of the esc apparatus genes named by Pallen 2005. LEE conserved in C. rodentium ICC168 (Petty 2010).

machinery_genes.quote: Components of the secretion apparatus that had obvious homologues in the better-characterized plasmid-encoded Ysc-Yop T3SS of Yersinia sp. were given the prefix Esc and the same suffix as their Yersinia homologues
machinery_genes.note: EscS = SctS-family export-apparatus gate (YscS homologue), one of the esc apparatus genes named by Pallen 2005. LEE conserved in C. rodentium ICC168 (Petty 2010).

machinery_genes.quote: Components of the secretion apparatus that had obvious homologues in the better-characterized plasmid-encoded Ysc-Yop T3SS of Yersinia sp. were given the prefix Esc and the same suffix as their Yersinia homologues
machinery_genes.note: EscT = SctT-family export-apparatus gate (YscT homologue), one of the esc apparatus genes named by Pallen 2005. LEE conserved in C. rodentium ICC168 (Petty 2010).

machinery_genes.quote: Components of the secretion apparatus that had obvious homologues in the better-characterized plasmid-encoded Ysc-Yop T3SS of Yersinia sp. were given the prefix Esc and the same suffix as their Yersinia homologues
machinery_genes.note: EscU = SctU-family export-gate autoprotease/switch (YscU homologue), one of the esc apparatus genes named by Pallen 2005. LEE conserved in C. rodentium ICC168 (Petty 2010).

machinery_genes.quote: A biochemical analysis was performed on three components: EscN, a predicted cytoplasmic ATPase; EscV, a predicted inner membrane protein; and EscC, a predicted outer membrane secretin.
machinery_genes.note: EscV = SctV-family major export-apparatus ring (YscV/InvA homologue). Named as the inner-membrane component in Gauthier 2003 (EPEC LEE) and listed among the esc apparatus genes by Pallen 2005. LEE conserved in C. rodentium ICC168 (Petty 2010).

machinery_genes.quote: the needle protein EscF and the putative inner rod component rOrf8 are also secreted
machinery_genes.note: Named directly as the C. rodentium needle protein in the secretome paper (Deng 2010). EscF = SctF needle subunit; biochemically confirmed as the needle component in EPEC by Gauthier 2003.

machinery_genes.quote: the needle protein EscF and the putative inner rod component rOrf8 are also secreted
machinery_genes.note: rOrf8 (also designated EscI) = SctI-family inner rod (YscI/PrgJ/MxiI homologue). Named as putative inner rod in the C. rodentium secretome paper (Deng 2010); homology to YscI/PrgJ/MxiI and the EscI designation established by Pallen 2005 (PMID 15757514).

machinery_genes.quote: most of the SepL sequence (up to residue 267) is homologous to YopN
machinery_genes.note: SepL = SctW-family gatekeeper, homologous to Yersinia YopN-TyeA (Pallen 2005, EPEC LEE). Controls the switch from translocator to effector secretion. LEE conserved in C. rodentium ICC168 (Petty 2010).

machinery_genes.role: cytoplasmic C-ring / sorting platform (FliN-domain; YscQ homologue)
machinery_genes.quote: a FliN domain (COG1886) in the C-terminal half of SepQ ... should be re-named EscQ
machinery_genes.note: SepQ (proposed re-name EscQ) = SctQ-family cytoplasmic C-ring / sorting-platform component, YscQ homologue (Pallen 2005). LEE conserved in C. rodentium ICC168 (Petty 2010).

machinery_genes.role: translocon filament sheath connecting needle to host membrane
machinery_genes.quote: confirmed the type III secretion of the translocators EspA, EspB, and EspD
machinery_genes.note: EspA forms the filamentous sheath extending the needle; named as a translocator (apparatus, not effector) in the C. rodentium secretome paper (Deng 2010). Included per the brief's rule that translocon proteins are apparatus.

machinery_genes.quote: confirmed the type III secretion of the translocators EspA, EspB, and EspD
machinery_genes.note: EspB = host-membrane translocon pore subunit (also has effector activity), named as a translocator in the C. rodentium secretome paper (Deng 2010). Listed as apparatus (translocon) per the brief.

machinery_genes.quote: confirmed the type III secretion of the translocators EspA, EspB, and EspD
machinery_genes.note: EspD = host-membrane translocon pore subunit, named as a translocator in the C. rodentium secretome paper (Deng 2010). Listed as apparatus (translocon) per the brief.

notes: IMPORTANT STRAIN/LOCATION CAVEAT: Citrobacter rodentium ICC168 has a single functional T3SS injectisome, the LEE-encoded (locus of enterocyte effacement) system. The benchmark label 'prophage_CRP4' refers to where this instance's effector ROD_25861 sits (a prophage region), NOT to a separate injectisome; the LEE apparatus and the prophage-borne effector are physically distant on the chromosome. There is no T3SS structural apparatus encoded within prophage CRP4. C. rodentium effectors are characteristically scattered across prophages/mobile elements while the single shared machinery is LEE-encoded (Petty 2010: 'the locus for enterocyte effacement (LEE) region, which encodes a T3SS and effector proteins'). The apparatus genes listed here are therefore the LEE injectisome components. Naming sources: (1) Deng 2010 C. rodentium type III secretome paper gives C. rodentium-specific quotes for EscN (ATPase), EscF (needle), rOrf8/EscI (inner rod), and the EspA/EspB/EspD translocators. (2) Pallen 2005 establishes the Esc-prefix convention and the full esc apparatus gene set (EscC, EscD, EscJ, EscN, EscR, EscS, EscT, EscU, EscV) plus SepL (gatekeeper, YopN-TyeA homologue) and SepQ/EscQ (C-ring, YscQ homologue) for the EPEC LEE. (3) Gauthier 2003 gives biochemical naming of EscC (OM secretin), EscV (IM), EscN (ATPase), EscF (needle) for EPEC LEE. (4) Petty 2010 establishes the LEE is conserved in C. rodentium ICC168 with ROD locus tags (LEE region ROD_29641-ROD_30191), licensing the strain transfer of the EPEC E2348/69 apparatus nomenclature. Core injectisome fully covered: OM secretin (EscC), IM rings (EscD, EscJ), export gate (EscR, EscS, EscT, EscU), major export ring (EscV), ATPase (EscN), C-ring (SepQ/EscQ), inner rod (rOrf8/EscI), needle (EscF), gatekeeper (SepL), translocon (EspA/EspB/EspD). DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED as non-machinery: secreted effectors including this instance's ROD_25861 and the LEE effectors Tir, Map, EspF, EspG, EspH, EspZ; the intimin adhesin (Eae) and translocated intimin receptor (Tir); secretion chaperones (CesT, CesD, CesAB); and transcriptional regulators (Ler/LEE-encoded regulator, GrlA/GrlR). SepD is named in Deng 2010 (delta-sepD mutant) as a T3SS gatekeeper-associated component but I list SepL as the canonical SctW gatekeeper with the strongest naming quote; SepD could be added as a second gatekeeper-associated component if desired (lower-confidence, role partially overlapping). The EPEC-strain origin of the Pallen and Gauthier apparatus nomenclature is the only strain transfer; gene content is identical in the conserved C. rodentium LEE per Petty 2010.
